description: Go 1.22+ high-performance AI proxy gateway refactored from 9router (Next.js)

phases.name: Core Infrastructure & Database Layer
phases.description: Project structure, SQLite persistence, config management, graceful shutdown
tasks: Restructure Go project layout (cmd/, internal/)
Implement SQLite database layer with migrations (schema.sql tables)
Implement settings repository (JSON blob store)
Implement KV store
Implement graceful shutdown with signal handling
Add structured logging (slog) with request context

phases.name: Provider & Connection Management
phases.description: Provider registry, connection CRUD, account fallback with exponential backoff
tasks: Implement provider nodes repository (custom compatible endpoints)
Implement account fallback logic (cooldown, exponential backoff, model locks)
Implement credential selection (priority-based, exclude set, cooldown-aware)
Implement connection status management (active/error/rate-limited)
Implement provider CRUD API endpoints (update/delete)

phases.name: Model Resolution & Combos
phases.description: Model string parsing, alias resolution, combo fallback strategies
tasks: Implement combo system (fallback + round-robin strategies)
Implement combo sticky round-robin limit
Implement model availability and disabled models
Implement combo CRUD API enhancements (update)
Add unit tests for model parsing and combo logic

phases.name: OpenAI-Compatible API Surface
tasks: Implement middleware chain (logging, recovery, CORS)
Implement /v1/messages endpoint (Anthropic-compatible passthrough)
Enhance SSE streaming proxy (disconnect-aware, [DONE] handling)
Implement request/response format translation (OpenAI<->Claude<->Gemini)
Implement usage extraction from upstream responses
Add integration tests with mock upstream

phases.name: Dashboard Panel System
phases.description: Embedded single-HTML panel (Vue3+Tailwind CDN), local mount, runtime download, CLI params
tasks: Implement CLI params: -host, -port, -db, -dashboard, -panel-url, -secret (all with defaults)
Implement env var support (CYRENE_HOST, CYRENE_PORT, etc.), flag takes priority
Implement embedded single-HTML dashboard (Vue3 + TailwindCSS via CDN, go:embed)
Implement panel serving: local dir (-dashboard) > downloaded cache > embedded fallback
Implement runtime panel download from -panel-url with cache in data dir
Implement panel routes (GET / serves dashboard, static file serving)
Panel features: provider list, connection status, alias management, basic usage view

phases.name: Authentication & API Keys
phases.description: Local API key management, Bearer token validation, dashboard auth
tasks: Implement API key generation (HMAC-signed)
Implement API key validation middleware for /v1/* routes
Implement dashboard session auth (cookie-based, -secret param)
Implement login/logout endpoints
Implement requireApiKey setting enforcement

phases.name: Usage Tracking & Observability
phases.description: Token usage recording, daily aggregation, request details logging
tasks: Implement usage history repository
Implement usage extraction from provider responses (stream + non-stream)
Implement daily usage aggregation (usageDaily table)
Implement request details logging (requestDetails table)
Implement /api/usage/* dashboard endpoints

phases.name: Dashboard Management API
phases.description: Complete REST API for all management operations
tasks: Implement /api/providers update/delete endpoints
Implement /api/provider-nodes CRUD endpoints
Implement /api/combos update endpoint
Implement /api/proxy-pools CRUD endpoints
Implement /api/health enhanced (db status, uptime, connection count)

phases.name: Advanced Features & Enhancements
phases.description: Token refresh, loop guard (VansRouter), proxy pools, provider testing
tasks: Implement OAuth token refresh (pre-check + on-401 retry)
Implement loop guard detection (VansRouter enhancement)
Implement termination prompt injection
Implement proxy pool support (outbound proxy rotation)
Implement provider connection testing endpoint
Implement max_tokens clamping for specific providers (NVIDIA Kimi)

phases.name: Maintenance Mode (post-v1.0.0)
phases.description: Perpetual: issues, deps, upstream sync (9router/VansRouter), provider registry updates
tasks: Check GitHub Issues for bug reports or feature requests
Run go build ./... && go test ./... to verify project health
Review and merge dependabot PRs if safe
Fix reported issues and push patches (tag v1.0.x)
Upstream sync: compare upstream_commits hashes, analyze new commits for borrowable improvements
Check 9router open-sse/providers/registry/ for new providers or baseUrl changes
If upstream has relevant changes: port to Go, commit, push
If no action needed: report 'no pending work' and exit

upstream_notes.decolua/9router: primary upstream, sync every maintenance cycle
upstream_notes.Vanszs/VansRouter: fork of 9router, reference only for unique enhancements (loop guard, termination prompt). Do NOT regular-diff — would duplicate 9router analysis.
